ztRemoves a qemu monitor event callback. De-registering for a callback
       will disable delivery of this event type

zpAdds a qemu monitor event callback. Registering for a monitor

z1virConnectDomainQemuMonitorEventRegister() failed)

Z(virConnectDomainQemuMonitorEventRegisterr

qemuMonitorEventRegister3
z%Send a Guest Agent command to domain Nz"virDomainQemuAgentCommand() failed)
virDomainQemuAgentCommandr
domain
timeoutr
qemuAgentCommandH
This API is QEMU specific, so it will only work with hypervisor
    connections to the QEMU driver.
    
    This API will attach to an externally launched QEMU process
    identified by @pid. There are several requirements to successfully
    attach to an external QEMU process:
    
      - It must have been started with a monitor socket using the UNIX
        domain socket protocol.
      - No device hotplug/unplug, or other configuration changes can
        have been made via the monitor since it started.
      - The '-name' and '-uuid' arguments should have been set (not
        mandatory, but strongly recommended)
    
    To date, the only platforms we know of where pid_t is larger than
    unsigned int (64-bit Windows) also lack UNIX sockets, so the choice
    of @pid_value as an unsigned int should not present any difficulties.
    
    If successful, then the guest will appear in the list of running
    domains for this connection, and other APIs should operate
    normally (provided the above requirements were honored). Nz
virDomainQemuAttach() failedr
virDomainQemuAttachr
Z	pid_valuer
__tmpr
qemuAttachN
zASend an arbitrary monitor command through qemu monitor of domain Nz$virDomainQemuMonitorCommand() failed)
virDomainQemuMonitorCommandr
qemuMonitorCommandi
sysr
ImportError
exc_infoZ
lib_eZ
cygvirtmod_qemuZ
cyg_e
countr
Z4VIR_CONNECT_DOMAIN_QEMU_MONITOR_EVENT_REGISTER_REGEXZ5VIR_CONNECT_DOMAIN_QEMU_MONITOR_EVENT_REGISTER_NOCASEZ#VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_AGENT_COMMAND_BLOCKZ!VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_AGENT_COMMAND_MINZ%VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_AGENT_COMMAND_DEFAULTZ$VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_AGENT_COMMAND_NOWAITZ&VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_AGENT_COMMAND_SHUTDOWNZ'VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_MONITOR_COMMAND_DEFAULTZ#VIR_DOMAIN_QEMU_MONITOR_COMMAND_HMPr
<module>
